compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the FOUNDATION story (every other story builds on it). It FULLY covers its owned slice:
- Parent AC2 clause "...using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" → fe57 AC1 (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search+comment+issuelinks+parent; no tokens/emails/accountIds) + AC2 (FakeAcliClient serves them through the production fetcher enrichment path with NO shape transformation) + AC4 (fixtures consumed only via the production path; no test hand-reshapes). The round-trip contract test that consumes them is correctly delegated to sibling 8937 (which declares depends_on fe57); fe57 explicitly scopes that test OUT. FULLY contributes the real-payload-fixture half of AC2.
- Parent SC1 ("Producer (fetcher+enrichment) and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → fe57 supplies the hermetic-but-honest fixtures + verified-fake foundation flowing through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment, eliminating the bug-0ee6 root cause (differs fed hand-built shapes the real fetcher never emits). Reinforced by 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake shape-fidelity dual-run). FULLY contributes (foundation half).
- Parent AC1 (SSOT snapshot-entry schema) → fe57 feeds the producer path that 8937's schema pins; schema artifact owned by 8937. Contributes.

Criteria this child does NOT own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC1 schema → 8937; AC2 round-trip test → 8937; AC3/SC2 (collapse dual appliers) → 33d0; AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary) → 2359; AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards) → 822a; verified-fake honesty/dual-run check → 59ef. fe57's declared OUT items (contract test → 8937; verified-fake honesty check → 59ef) are consistent with the roster. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child fe57 itself; its owned criteria (fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc) are coherent and fully covered.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC2 (real-payload-fixtures half) + SC1 (foundation half) FULLY contributed by THIS child; AC1 contributed (schema owned by 8937); AC2 round-trip / AC3 / AC4 / AC5 / SC2 / SC3 / SC4 covered by named siblings; SC5/AC7 anti-change-detector posture applied per-test across roster; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already tracked on siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937 — restated here for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child, and confirmed by a repo search: no documented anti-change-detector guardrail artifact for reconciler tests exists today, and no child produces one — docs/mutation-testing.md is a general mutation-testing doc, not the AC6 governing artifact, and no child references producing/extending it):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN deliverable; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child fe57 only references the anti-change-detector concern as "a documented re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors" — self-scoped to its own fixtures, not a governing guardrail for new reconciler tests; siblings 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 8937 AC4, 59ef AC3 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; repo search for an existing reconciler anti-change-detector guardrail returned no governing artifact (only general docs/mutation-testing.md). No sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests.
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child fe57 itself for its owned criteria (fixtures/fake foundation fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked across the sibling set.

comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child; no per-criterion coverage gap attributable to fe57 itself:
- Parent AC2's fixtures half ("One round-trip contract test … using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads") → fe57 AC1 (`tests/fixtures/jira/*.json` hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search + comment + issuelinks + parent) + AC2 (`FakeAcliClient` serves the fixtures through the production fetcher enrichment path with NO shape transformation) + AC3 (live-gated `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` + documented re-capture cadence) + AC4 (fixtures consumed only via the production code path; no test hand-reshapes). The round-trip CONTRACT itself is correctly delegated to keystone 8937 (which depends_on fe57). FULLY at roster level.
- Parent SC1 ("Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → fe57 OWNS the foundational "hermetic-but-honest" half: real-payload fixtures + a fake that adds NO shape massaging, so the differs are no longer fed hand-built dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits (the exact 0ee6/3f04 root cause). Reinforced by 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity). FULLY.
- Parent AC1's intent (the shared snapshot shape stays honest at the producer/enrichment source) → contributed by fe57's no-massage fake; the SSOT schema artifact itself is 8937 AC1. 

DELEGATION — criteria fe57 does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; fe57's declared OUT items ("the contract test that consumes them → separate story" = 8937; "the verified-fake honesty check → separate story" = 59ef) are consistent with the roster:
- AC1 schema SSOT + AC2 round-trip contract → 8937 (keystone) + 59ef (verified-fake fidelity).
- AC3/SC2 (collapse dual outbound appliers; delete retired path) → 33d0.
- AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) → 2359.
- AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip; de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards) → 822a.
- AC7 (all children closed; suite green; no net brittle increase) → structural/aggregate epic roll-up.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: fixtures half of AC2 + SC1 (honest-fixtures foundation) FULLY by THIS child; AC1 intent contributed (schema artifact → 8937); round-trip contract → 8937; AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; SC5 posture applied per-artifact (fe57's re-capture doc guards its own fixtures vs change-detector rot); AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already tracked across siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937 — restated here for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture (or, for fe57, guards its OWN fixtures from rotting into change-detectors via the re-capture doc) to its OWN deliverable; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: fe57's "documented re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors" is self-scoped to THIS story's own fixtures; 8937 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 2359 AC4, 822a AC4, and 59ef AC3 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture/guard applied per-artifact, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child fe57 itself for its owned criteria (fixtures half of AC2 + SC1 foundation fully covered); the single absence above is the only uncovered parent criterion across the whole roster (AC6), already tracked across the sibling set.

OWNED CRITERIA — fe57's slice is FULLY covered; no per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to this child:
- fe57 is the FOUNDATION story: scrubbed real-Jira payload fixtures (search + comment + issuelinks + parent) under tests/fixtures/jira/, plus FakeAcliClient serving them through the production fetcher.fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape massaging, plus a live-gated capture/scrub script + re-capture doc.
- This directly supplies the "fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" half of parent AC2 (the round-trip contract test that consumes them is correctly delegated to 8937, which depends_on fe57) and the "real Jira shapes" substrate of parent SC4. It also underpins parent AC1/SC1 (honest producer shape at the source) and 59ef's verified-fake fidelity check.
- fe57's declared OUT items (contract test → 8937; verified-fake honesty check → 59ef) are consistent with the roster.

DELEGATION — criteria fe57 does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings: AC1 schema SSOT + AC2 round-trip contract / SC1 → 8937; AC3/SC2 (collapse dual appliers) → 33d0; AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) → 2359; AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards) → 822a; verified-fake honesty → 59ef.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC2 fixtures-half + SC4 substrate FULLY by THIS child; AC1/SC1 contributed; AC1(schema)/AC2(contract)/AC3/AC4/AC5/SC1/SC2/SC3 covered by named siblings; SC5 posture clause applied per-test; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence, below).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; re-cross-checked against the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child; already tracked across siblings 8937/59ef/33d0/2359/822a — restated here for this pairing's record):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including fe57, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests/fixtures; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact governing FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: fe57's "re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors" is self-scoped to THIS story's own fixtures; 8937 AC4, 59ef AC3, 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 — e.g. add an AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child fe57 itself for its owned criteria (fixtures + fake foundation fully covered); the single absence above is the only uncovered parent criterion across the whole roster (AC6).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration of fe57's premise (the fake's surface is real and flows through the PRODUCTION enrichment, not a parallel path):
- The four enrichment methods fe57's FakeAcliClient targets all exist on the real client: search_issues (acli.py L370), get_comment_map (acli_graph.py L289), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py L391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py L189).
- The production snapshot builder constructs `client = acli_mod.AcliClient(...)` where `acli_mod = _load_acli()` (fetcher.py L294-298), and the code comment there explicitly notes "tests swap with a fake AcliClient-only module" — this IS the documented injection seam fe57 relies on. The enrichment then calls client.get_parent_map (fetcher.py L364-365), client.get_issuelinks_map (L450-451), and the comment map, merging `parent` as {"key":…} (L369), `issuelinks` (list), and the nested `comment` field into each snapshot entry via fetch_snapshot → _build_snapshot. So a no-massage FakeAcliClient does flow through the real fetch_snapshot enrichment exactly as fe57 AC2/AC4 assert.

fe57 file_impact (tests/fixtures/jira/ [new], tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py [new FakeAcliClient], scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py [new]) is DISJOINT from every sibling's edits: 8937 (_snapshot_schema.py, test_snapshot_contract.py, fetcher.py), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). fe57 is the FOUNDATION story; 8937, 59ef, and 822a each declare depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57's three files are net-new and disjoint from all siblings. The FakeAcliClient is PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 59ef/8937/822a — a clean producer/consumer split, not a shared mutable write. The _load_acli()/acli_mod injection seam (fetcher.py L294-298) is consumed, not co-edited.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 8937 (snapshot-entry schema targets exactly those unmassaged shapes), 59ef AC2 (a fixture-faithful, unmassaged fake is precisely what passes the return-shape contract), and 822a (round-trips those shapes). All four referenced enrichment methods exist on the real client — no fictional surface.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the root foundation needing no sibling input, so it correctly declares no depends_on; the three downstream consumers correctly declare depends_on fe57. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc and explicitly scopes OUT the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef), consistent with the roster. Distinct surface from every sibling (fixtures/fake vs schema vs telemetry vs applier-collapse vs probe).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures/fake must exist before the tests that consume them) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges. fe57 is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The fake adds NO shape massaging (AC2/AC4), so consumers receive exactly the production-fetcher shapes; this ENABLES rather than constrains the siblings. The re-capture doc (AC3) guards the fixtures against change-detector rot, consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests."
- Residual references — BENIGN. fe57 references only live producer/consumer surfaces (all four enrichment methods + the fetch_snapshot seam verified present). 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf outbound applier strands nothing fe57 references (fe57 drives the INBOUND fetch/enrichment path, not the outbound typed leaf).

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 COVERAGE matter already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits — it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration of fe57's premises (the foundation story's fixtures+fake are grounded in real surfaces, not fictional):
- All four enrichment methods fe57 fakes resolve on the real client with the exact signatures referenced: get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py L289), get_issuelinks_map (L391), get_parent_map (L189), search_issues (acli.py L370). The fake returning these from "the real method signatures" is accurate.
- fetcher.fetch_snapshot (fetcher.py L480) merges the enrichment maps into snapshot entries through the production path exactly as fe57 describes: comment field merged as the nested raw Jira dict {"comments":[...]} (L413-417; get_comment_map docstring L303-308 confirms the raw nested shape — the bug-0ee6 seam), issuelinks list merged (L450-454; bug-3f04 enrichment), parent map merged. So "the fake adds NO shape massaging; fixtures flow through production fetch_snapshot enrichment" is realizable against current code.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57's file_impact (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json, tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py, scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py + re-capture doc) is net-new; no sibling co-edits these. 59ef adds test_acliclient_contract.py (and only READS acli_graph.py), 8937 adds _snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py + fetcher.py producer-validates, 822a adds test_roundtrip.py + edits the probe script, 33d0/2359 edit the outbound applier cluster. fe57 only READS the production fetcher/enrichment surface through the fake; no shared mutable file state.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 8937's snapshot-entry schema (the unmassaged fixtures are precisely the shapes the schema targets), 59ef AC2 (an unmassaged fixture-faithful fake is exactly what passes the shape contract), and 822a's behavioral round-trip. No representation contradiction. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry mismatch tracked on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal and untouched by fe57's inbound-enrichment fixtures/fake.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the declared FOUNDATION ("the foundation every other story builds on"); the three consumer siblings each declare depends_on fe57 (8937, 59ef, 822a). fe57 itself correctly depends on nothing — it is the root producer of fixtures+fake. It scopes the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef) explicitly OUT, consistent with the roster. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns the fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script; 59ef explicitly scopes "capturing the fixtures (story A)" OUT and consumes the fake; 8937 explicitly scopes "capturing fixtures (story A)" OUT and consumes them; 822a uses "story A's fixtures/fake" and scopes the schema OUT. Clean producer/consumer split — fe57 PRODUCES the artifact each sibling CONSUMES; no duplicated deliverable.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The hard ordering edge (fixtures+fake must exist before any consuming test) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges. fe57's files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster, so it is not entangled in that hazard.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. fe57 is purely additive (new fixtures, fake, capture script, doc). Its AC4 ("consumed only via the production code path; no test reads them and hand-reshapes") and the documented re-capture cadence ("so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") align with parent AC7 ("no net increase in brittle tests") and the epic's anti-change-detector posture. It imposes no brittle expectation on any consumer sibling.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four faked enrichment method signatures exist on the real client (verified above), and the production fetch_snapshot merge points are live. 33d0 deletes the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier, NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface fe57 fakes, so its deletion strands nothing fe57 references. The secret-scrubbing/capture script targets live Jira behind a gate (no committed-secret hazard for siblings).

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (a documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 COVERAGE matter already tracked across the sibling set (33d0/2359/59ef/822a/8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here. (Note: fe57's re-capture doc "so fixtures do not rot into change-detectors" is self-scoped to its own fixtures, not the governing artifact AC6 requires — consistent with the G3 finding, not a new G4 issue.)

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility): PASS / not-applicable. The plan does not assert any new, unverified external capability. The Jira REST endpoints it relies on are already in production use in this codebase (e.g. `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` with `fields=["issuelinks"]` and `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}?fields=issuelinks` in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py`), and live auth/HTTPS preconditions (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN) are already established by the existing external suite (`tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`). The plan's strategy is deliberately *hermetic* (fixtures captured from real Jira, served through an in-process fake), with full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact explicitly out of scope — so it adds no new live external dependency. No findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding attributable to this child; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED / CONTRIBUTED by THIS child (fe57 = the foundation: scrubbed real-Jira fixtures + FakeAcliClient + live-gated capture/scrub script + re-capture doc):
- Parent AC "One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, USING FIXTURES DERIVED FROM REAL JIRA PAYLOADS" → fe57 OWNS the "fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" half: fe57 AC1 (tests/fixtures/jira/*.json hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search+comment+issuelinks+parent) + AC2 (FakeAcliClient serves them through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation) + AC4 (fixtures consumed only via the production path, no hand-reshaping). The round-trip TEST itself is correctly delegated to 8937 (story B, which declares depends_on fe57). FULLY covered jointly (fixtures here + test at 8937).
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" → fe57 OWNS the HONESTY-of-input half: hermetic-but-honest fixtures served unmassaged through the production path remove the bug-0ee6 root cause (differs fed hand-built shapes the real fetcher never emits). Complemented by 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity, which declares depends_on fe57). CONTRIBUTED.
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → fe57 AC3 (live-gated capture + documented re-capture cadence "so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors"). CONTRIBUTED.

DELEGATION — parent criteria fe57 does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; fe57's declared OUT items (the contract test → story B/8937; the verified-fake honesty check → story C/59ef) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "fetcher snapshot validated vs schema" + AC "each differ's reads validated vs schema" + AC "round-trip contract test through production path" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3). fe57 supplies the fixtures these consume.
- AC "Outbound collapsed to a single production path" + AC "retired outbound applier path deleted" + SC "outbound exercised through the production path, not a dead path" → 33d0.
- AC "outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first)" + SC "silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary" → 2359.
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a (which reuses fe57's fixtures, depends_on fe57).
- AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 (story B), per its description ("Owns the epic's guardrail artifact … commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it") and 8937 AC5. Parent AC explicitly NAMES story B as owner; under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts) this is FULLY covered. The recurring "AC6 PARTIALLY covered" finding raised on sibling tickets earlier is a FALSE POSITIVE (paraphrased "governs new tests" wording not matching the plan text, predating 8937's explicit ownership) and was already withdrawn on 2359/33d0/59ef/822a; not applicable to this pairing.

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- FULLY (fe57 owns its half; jointly complete): parent AC "round-trip contract uses fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" (fixtures → fe57; test → 8937).
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape" (honest-input half), parent SC "zero change-detector / lean suite" (re-capture cadence keeps fixtures from rotting).
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: SSOT schema + fetcher/differ validation (8937); applier collapse + retired-path deletion (33d0); sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359); bidirectional hermetic round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); verified-fake fidelity (59ef); documented anti-change-detector guardrail (8937, story B — named owner).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green".
- UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. fe57's owned share (real-payload fixtures + unmassaged FakeAcliClient through the production path + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc) is the foundation that three siblings (8937, 59ef, 822a) explicitly depend_on; the union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and no parent criterion is left uncovered because of this child.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of fe57's premises (the fixtures+fake are grounded in real surfaces, not fictional, and flow through the PRODUCTION enrichment, not a parallel path):
- fetcher.fetch_snapshot exists (fetcher.py:480) and merges the enrichment maps into snapshot entries through the production path exactly as fe57 describes: comment field via client.get_comment_map (fetcher.py:402,413-414), plus issuelinks and parent enrichment (fetcher.py:380-472).
- The enrichment-method signatures fe57's FakeAcliClient targets are real and match: get_comment_map(self, project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289); get_issuelinks_map mirrors it (acli_graph.py:409). The existing production test fakes ALREADY swap a fake client with these signatures through the production fetch_snapshot path (e.g. tests/unit/.../mutate/test_comment_enrichment.py:196,206; test_link_sync.py:163,176) — confirming the injection seam fe57 relies on is real and in use.

File-impact disjointness: fe57 = tests/fixtures/jira/ (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py (new FakeAcliClient), scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py (new) + re-capture doc. DISJOINT from every sibling's edits — 8937 (_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh/test_roundtrip.py). fe57 only READS the production fetch/enrichment surface through the fake. Three siblings (8937, 59ef, 822a) declare depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57's three files are net-new and disjoint from all siblings. The FakeAcliClient is PRODUCED by fe57 and CONSUMED (read) by 59ef/8937/822a — a clean producer/consumer split over declared depends_on edges, not a shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") is mutually reinforcing with 8937 (snapshot-entry schema targets exactly those unmassaged shapes), 59ef AC2 (an unmassaged fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes the return-shape contract), and 822a (round-trips those shapes). fe57 covers the INBOUND fetch/enrichment surface; the outbound labels_applied list-vs-count representation conflict tracked on the 33d0↔2359 pairing is outbound-internal and untouched by fe57 (fe57's inbound fixtures/fake neither assert nor depend on labels_applied).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. fe57 is the root foundation needing no sibling input, so it correctly declares no depends_on; the three downstream consumers correctly declare depends_on fe57. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc, and explicitly scopes OUT the contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake honesty check (→59ef), consistent with the roster. Distinct surface from every sibling (fixtures/fake vs schema vs telemetry vs applier-collapse vs probe).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures/fake must exist before the tests that consume them) is captured by the three declared depends_on fe57 edges. fe57 is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The fake adds NO shape massaging (AC2/AC4), so consumers receive exactly the production-fetcher shapes — this ENABLES rather than constrains siblings. The re-capture doc (AC3, "so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") guards the fixtures against change-detector rot, consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / parent "no net increase in brittle tests."
- Residual references — BENIGN. fe57 references only live inbound fetch/enrichment surfaces (all four enrichment methods + the fetch_snapshot merge points verified present). 33d0's deletion of the DEAD OUTBOUND typed-leaf applier strands nothing fe57 references (fe57 drives the inbound fetch/enrichment path, not the outbound typed leaf). The secret-scrubbing/capture script targets live Jira behind a gate (no committed-secret hazard for siblings).

NOTE on the one real cross-child G4 finding in this epic (outbound labels_applied list-vs-count representation conflict): it is correctly localized to the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier-payload pairing. fe57's inbound fixtures/fake do not assert or depend on labels_applied, so it does not implicate this pairing.

NOTE on the parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion: per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause it is owned by 8937 — a G3 coverage matter, not a G4 cross-child inconsistency; not addressed here. (fe57's re-capture doc is self-scoped to its own fixtures, consistent with that, not a new G4 issue.)

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

fe57 is the FOUNDATION: it PRODUCES tests/fixtures/jira/ + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py (FakeAcliClient) + scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py + re-capture doc. Siblings 8937, 59ef, 822a all declare depends_on fe57 and CONSUME these artifacts — clean producer/consumer split with declared edges.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. fe57 is the sole PRODUCER of the fixtures + FakeAcliClient; 8937 (test_snapshot_contract.py), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py), 822a (test_roundtrip.py) only READ them. No sibling co-writes tests/fixtures/jira/ or _fakes.py; no conflicting shared-file mutation.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake adds NO shape massaging; serves through production fetch_snapshot enrichment") is mutually reinforcing with 59ef AC2 (the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client) and with 8937's snapshot-entry schema — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is exactly what those siblings require. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter tracked on 2359/33d0 is outbound-applier-internal; fe57's inbound enrichment fixtures/fake neither touch nor depend on it.

Its declared OUT items (the contract test → 8937; the verified-fake honesty check → 59ef) are present as named siblings.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. fe57 owns fixtures + fake + capture script + re-capture doc; it explicitly scopes OUT the consuming contract test (→8937) and the verified-fake fidelity harness (→59ef). 59ef VERIFIES the fake fe57 BUILDS; 8937 CONSUMES the fixtures fe57 CAPTURES — complementary, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering constraint (foundation before its consumers) is captured by 8937/59ef/822a each declaring depends_on fe57. fe57 itself is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface entirely).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. fe57's deliverables are additive test scaffolding; AC4 enforces consumption ONLY via the production code path (no test reads them and hand-reshapes), which actively supports the epic's anti-change-detector posture rather than imposing brittle expectations. Secret-scrubbing (AC1/AC3) keeps committed fixtures safe.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every method the FakeAcliClient mirrors exists on the real client (verified above). 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier paths (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface fe57 fixtures exercise, so its deletion strands nothing fe57 references. No dangling reference.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail matter is a G3 coverage question (and per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × fe57): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child fe57 "Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding attributable to this child; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED / CONTRIBUTED by THIS child (fe57 = the foundation: fixtures captured from real Jira + verified fake client served through the production path):
- Parent AC "One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" → fe57 CONTRIBUTES the fixture/fake HALF: fe57 AC1 (`tests/fixtures/jira/*.json` hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search + comment + issuelinks + parent), AC2 (`FakeAcliClient` serves the fixtures through the production fetcher enrichment path with NO shape transformation), AC3 (live-gated capture+scrub script + documented re-capture cadence), AC4 (fixtures consumed only via the production code path; no test reads them and hand-reshapes). The round-trip TEST itself is correctly delegated to keystone 8937 (8937 AC3, which declares depends_on fe57). FULLY covered as a pairing (fixtures/fake → fe57; test → 8937).
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" → fe57 owns the HONESTY-OF-THE-SOURCE half: fixtures captured from real Jira and served unmassaged through `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` are precisely what prevents the bug-0ee6 failure mode "differs unit-tested against hand-built snapshot dicts in shapes the real fetcher never emits." CONTRIBUTED, complementary to 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and 59ef (verified-fake fidelity that keeps fe57's fake honest).
- Parent SC "Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → fe57 supplies the real-shape fixtures/fake that 822a's hermetic round-trip reuses (822a declares depends_on fe57). CONTRIBUTED (fixture foundation).
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → fe57's documented re-capture procedure ("so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors") applies the anti-change-detector posture to its own artifacts. CONTRIBUTED.

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; fe57's declared OUT items (the contract test that consumes the fixtures → story B/8937; the verified-fake honesty check → story C/59ef) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "A single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module exists as the canonical shape" → 8937 AC1.
- AC "A test validates the fetcher's snapshot output against that schema" → 8937 AC2.
- AC "A test validates each differ's snapshot reads against that schema" → 8937 AC1 (schema covers every field the differs read) + AC3 (round-trip drives inbound_differ + outbound_differ reads against the schema-validated snapshot).
- AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" + AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" + SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0.
- AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout" + SC "A silent sub-op no-op … fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359.
- AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" → 822a.
- AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 (story B): 8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact … commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it" + 8937 AC5. The parent AC NAMES its owner verbatim; under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts) this is FULLY covered.

ANTI-FP / the recurring "documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered — no sibling produces a governing artifact" finding (raised against fe57 and siblings in early audits) is a FALSE POSITIVE and stays WITHDRAWN. It was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text; the actual parent AC explicitly names story B (= 8937) as owner and is delivered by 8937's description + AC5. The same false positive has already been withdrawn across siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a and on 8937.

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- FULLY / CONTRIBUTED (owned share of THIS child): parent AC "round-trip contract test … using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" (fixture/fake half — fe57 AC1/AC2/AC3/AC4; test half → 8937); parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape" (honest-source half); parent SC "bidirectional round-trips for real Jira shapes" (fixture foundation reused by 822a); parent SC "zero change-detector / lean" (re-capture honesty).
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: SSOT schema + fetcher/differ schema validation + round-trip test (8937); applier collapse + retired-path deletion + production-path exercise (33d0); sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359); hermetic bidirectional round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); verified-fake fidelity (59ef); documented anti-change-detector guardrail (8937, named owner).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "All six child stories are closed; the full reconciler suite is green."
- PARTIALLY / UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. fe57's owned share (real-Jira fixtures + FakeAcliClient served through the production fetcher enrichment with no shape massaging + live-gated capture/scrub script + re-capture doc) is fully delivered and is the foundation the round-trip (8937), verified-fake honesty (59ef), and bidirectional probe (822a) build on. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; none is left uncovered by, or solely-and-uncovered on, this child.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 3 advisory). Remediating all 3 advisories in implementation: (1) add a committed secret-scan test over tests/fixtures/jira/*.json that fails on any token/email/accountId pattern (verifies AC1 deterministically); (2) FakeAcliClient routes fixtures only via fetcher.fetch_snapshot/_build_snapshot enrichment merge, never read+reshaped directly (AC2/AC4); (3) install the fake via the established seam — patch fetcher._load_acli to return a stub module whose AcliClient(**kwargs) factory yields the fake — so fixtures flow through the production parent/comment/issuelinks merge (fetcher.py), not by calling fake methods directly.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The escaped bugs all began with unit tests feeding the differs hand-built `jira_snapshot` dicts in shapes the real fetcher never produces (flat `comments` instead of nested `comment`; `issuelinks` the snapshot never carried). The converged fix (per `jira-cli`'s pattern) is **fixtures captured from real Jira payloads, served through the production serialization path** — never hand-massaged. This is the foundation every other story in the epic builds on.

## What

- Capture **scrubbed real Jira REST payloads** for the exact responses the fetcher consumes: the paged `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql`, plus the enrichment maps `get_comment_map` (`{"comment":{"comments":[…]}}`), `get_issuelinks_map` (`{"issuelinks":[…]}`), and `get_parent_map` (`{"parent":{"key":…}}`). Store under `tests/fixtures/jira/`.
- A `FakeAcliClient` (in `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py`) that returns those fixtures from the real method signatures (`search_issues`, `get_comment_map`, `get_issuelinks_map`, `get_parent_map`) so they flow through the production `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` enrichment — the fake adds NO shape massaging.
- Secret-scrubbing on capture (strip API tokens, emails, accountIds) via layered redaction (vcrpy-style), so committed fixtures carry no secrets.
- A `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` (live-gated) + documented re-capture procedure so fixtures stay honest and do not rot into change-detectors.

## Scope

- In: captured fixtures + the fake client + capture/scrub script + re-capture doc.
- Out: the contract test that consumes them (separate story), the verified-fake honesty check (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `tests/fixtures/jira/*.json` hold scrubbed real Jira payloads for search + comment + issuelinks + parent (verified: no tokens/emails/accountIds present).
- [ ] `FakeAcliClient` serves the fixtures through the production fetcher enrichment path with no shape transformation.
- [ ] `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` captures + scrubs from a live Jira (gated) and the re-capture cadence is documented.
- [ ] Fixtures are consumed only via the production code path (no test reads them and hand-reshapes).
compiled_state.title: Hermetic-but-honest Jira fixtures + verified fake client (served through the production path)

verify_commands.dd_text: Fixtures carry no secrets and the fake routes them through the production fetch path
